component_details.name: EVM Execution Client
component_details.description: Software responsible for executing smart contracts and managing state changes.

attack_surface.data_description: User input for vote submission (e.g., voter address, vote choice)

behavior_details.name: Bridge Tokens/NFTs
behavior_details.description: External User transfers assets via the Berachain Bridge.

data_flow_details.description: User initiates a transaction on the blockchain.

boundary_details.name: User Wallet to Berachain
boundary_details.description: Separates the user's local wallet environment from the core blockchain network.

security_gap.gap_description: The governance contract may not adequately verify the identity of the voter submitting the vote, potentially allowing a malicious user to impersonate another BGT holder or vote on their behalf.

actor_risk.description: An external user attempting to submit votes under a false identity.

actor_details.name: External User
actor_details.description: General user interacting with the Berachain ecosystem.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Compromised vote integrity due to insufficient identity verification can undermine governance outcomes.

assumptions: The mechanism for linking a vote to a specific BGT holder's voting power is flawed.
The governance contract accepts votes from an address without verifying the address controls the BGT or is a legitimate delegate.

mitigation.strategy: Implement cryptographic signatures for vote submissions linked to the BGT holding/delegating address.

description: A malicious user could spoof the identity of a legitimate BGT holder to cast unauthorized votes on governance proposals.

component_details.name: EVM Execution Client
component_details.description: Software responsible for executing smart contracts and managing state changes.

attack_surface.data_description: User input for vote submission (e.g., vote choice, voting power representation)

behavior_details.name: Bridge Tokens/NFTs
behavior_details.description: External User transfers assets via the Berachain Bridge.

data_flow_details.description: User initiates a transaction on the blockchain.

boundary_details.name: User Wallet to Berachain
boundary_details.description: Separates the user's local wallet environment from the core blockchain network.

security_gap.gap_description: The governance contract may not fully validate the integrity of the submitted vote data, allowing a malicious user to alter their vote choice or attempt to manipulate the voting power associated with their vote.

actor_risk.description: An external user attempting to modify vote data during submission.

actor_details.name: External User
actor_details.description: General user interacting with the Berachain ecosystem.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Tampering with vote data compromises the accuracy and fairness of governance decisions.

assumptions: The governance contract has insufficient input validation for vote parameters.
Maliciously crafted vote data is processed by the contract instead of being rejected.

mitigation.strategy: Implement rigorous input validation and data integrity checks within the governance contract for all vote parameters.

description: A malicious user could tamper with their vote data during submission to the governance contract, potentially altering their vote or misrepresenting their voting power.

component_details.name: EVM Execution Client
component_details.description: Software responsible for executing smart contracts and managing state changes.

attack_surface.data_description: On-chain record of the vote submission

behavior_details.name: Bridge Tokens/NFTs
behavior_details.description: External User transfers assets via the Berachain Bridge.

data_flow_details.description: User initiates a transaction on the blockchain.

boundary_details.name: User Wallet to Berachain
boundary_details.description: Separates the user's local wallet environment from the core blockchain network.

security_gap.gap_description: If the vote recording mechanism is flawed (e.g., uses an off-chain component without proper proof), a user might be able to deny having cast a vote after the fact.

actor_risk.description: An external user attempting to deny a previously performed action.

actor_details.name: External User
actor_details.description: General user interacting with the Berachain ecosystem.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Standard on-chain transactions are cryptographically linked to the sender, preventing repudiation, but potential off-chain flows could introduce this risk.

assumptions: The system relies on an off-chain component to record or prove votes.
The on-chain record of the vote is not sufficiently linked to the user's identity or signature.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure all votes are recorded on-chain via signed transactions, providing a non-repudiable record.

description: A malicious user could deny having cast a specific vote on a governance proposal if the vote recording mechanism is not fully non-repudiable.

component_details.name: EVM Execution Client
component_details.description: Software responsible for executing smart contracts and managing state changes.

attack_surface.data_description: Interaction with governance contract state and events

behavior_details.name: Bridge Tokens/NFTs
behavior_details.description: External User transfers assets via the Berachain Bridge.

data_flow_details.description: User initiates a transaction on the blockchain.

boundary_details.name: User Wallet to Berachain
boundary_details.description: Separates the user's local wallet environment from the core blockchain network.

security_gap.gap_description: The governance contract or related services might expose sensitive data, such as non-public proposal details, identities of anonymous voters, or voting strategies, to a user interacting with the voting function or querying public state/events.

actor_risk.description: An external user attempting to gain unauthorized access to confidential information.

actor_details.name: External User
actor_details.description: General user interacting with the Berachain ecosystem.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Blockchain transparency means most voting data is public, but accidental exposure of sensitive metadata is possible with flawed contract design.

assumptions: Sensitive data is stored on-chain or accessible via public contract interfaces/events.
The contract logic allows querying or inferring private information during or after the voting process.

mitigation.strategy: Avoid storing sensitive data on-chain; ensure contract interfaces only expose public information.

description: A malicious user could potentially gain unauthorized access to sensitive information related to governance by interacting with the voting contract.

component_details.name: EVM Execution Client
component_details.description: Software responsible for executing smart contracts and managing state changes.

attack_surface.data_description: Transaction volume and content submitted for voting

behavior_details.name: Bridge Tokens/NFTs
behavior_details.description: External User transfers assets via the Berachain Bridge.

data_flow_details.description: User initiates a transaction on the blockchain.

boundary_details.name: User Wallet to Berachain
boundary_details.description: Separates the user's local wallet environment from the core blockchain network.

security_gap.gap_description: The governance contract or underlying network may be vulnerable to resource exhaustion or disruption caused by a malicious user submitting excessive or malformed vote transactions, preventing legitimate users from voting or proposals from being processed.

actor_risk.description: An external user attempting to overload the system or cause failures.

actor_details.name: External User
actor_details.description: General user interacting with the Berachain ecosystem.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Disrupting the voting process can prevent governance decisions and harm system availability. While gas costs mitigate basic spam, smart contract vulnerabilities can enable targeted DoS.

assumptions: The governance contract or network has insufficient rate limiting or input validation to prevent DoS via voting transactions.
Processing malformed vote data causes excessive resource consumption or reverts.

mitigation.strategy: Implement gas limits, efficient contract logic, rigorous input validation, and monitor transaction volume for anomalies.

description: A malicious user could attempt to perform a denial-of-service attack on the governance voting process by flooding the network with transactions or submitting invalid votes.

component_details.name: EVM Execution Client
component_details.description: Software responsible for executing smart contracts and managing state changes.

attack_surface.data_description: Authorization and access control checks within the governance contract

behavior_details.name: Bridge Tokens/NFTs
behavior_details.description: External User transfers assets via the Berachain Bridge.

data_flow_details.description: User initiates a transaction on the blockchain.

boundary_details.name: User Wallet to Berachain
boundary_details.description: Separates the user's local wallet environment from the core blockchain network.

security_gap.gap_description: Flaws in the governance contract's authorization logic could allow a malicious user to vote with more power than their BGT holding/delegation entitles them to, or potentially perform other privileged actions like creating or executing proposals.

actor_risk.description: An external user attempting to perform actions or wield power beyond their authorized level.

actor_details.name: External User
actor_details.description: General user interacting with the Berachain ecosystem.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Exploiting authorization flaws can severely compromise the integrity and control of the governance system.

assumptions: The governance contract incorrectly calculates or verifies voting power.
Authorization checks for voting or other actions can be bypassed.

mitigation.strategy: Implement strict and thoroughly audited access control mechanisms and voting power calculations within the governance contract.

description: A malicious user could exploit vulnerabilities in the governance contract to elevate their privileges, such as voting with inflated power.
